results.title: Financial Key Ratios: EV/EBIT, EV/EBITA, EV/EBITDA, and EV/Sales
results.content: * EV-based multiples such as EV/EBITDA, EV/EBITA, EV/EBIT, and EV/Sales provide a comprehensive valuation of a company by considering its entire capital structure, making them useful for comparing companies with different leverage and asset profiles. * Each EV-based multiple serves different purposes: EV/EBIT is useful for assessing recurring operating profitability, EV/EBITDA is beneficial for cross-company comparisons by excluding D&A, and EV/EBITA neutralizes M&A history by adding back amortization of acquired intangibles. This article in our series on financial key ratios discusses the enterprise value-based multiples EV/EBITDA, EV/EBITA, EV/EBIT, and EV/Sales. That is why a company can trade at ~10x on an EV/EBITDA basis yet at a much richer ~18x on EV/EBIT once depreciation and amortization are recognized. **EV/EBIT:** Use this ratio when you want a read on recurring operating profitability that is capital structure neutral and still reflects the economic use of long-lived assets. The EV/Sales multiple helps investors measure value when profits are thin or negative since it compares the value of the whole company to revenue and bypasses earnings that can be below zero.
